---
title: Database Redundancy
subtitle: SpringBloom2021 - Genome-resolved Metaproteomic Dataset
date: 09.04.2025
author: Cyrill Hofer
output:
  rmdformats::readthedown:
    highlight: tango
    self_contained: true
    code_folding: hide
    thumbnails: true
    gallery: false
    fig_width: 15
    df_print: kable
    fig_caption: yes
    use_bookdown: true
    css: custom.css
---

# Data loading
```{r}
# Clear R's Brain
rm(list = ls())

# Set the working directory
setwd("/SynologyDrive/PhD/Projects/SpringBloom2021/DataAnalysis_SpringBloom2021/Figure_01/Database_Redundancy/")

# Libraries
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(scales)
library(patchwork)

# Color palette (optional; keep if you use it elsewhere)
source("ColorPalette.R")

# Load redundancy tables
species_dat <- read_delim("SpeciesLevelDatabaseRedundancy.tsv")
mag_dat     <- read_delim("MagLevelDatabaseRedundancy.tsv")

# Load gene annotation table (species-level)
gene_dat <- read.csv("../../../Data/Gene_Data/Final_Data/Gene_Table.csv")

# Load peptide inference reports
species_report_file <- "FGCZ_SpringBloom2021_95MagSpeciesDB_15JUL24.Reformated.ProteinInferenceReport.txt"
mag_report_file     <- "Springbloom2021_AllMags.ProteinInferenceReport.txt"
```

# Data manipulation
```{r}
# ---- Redundancy data (protein clustering) ----

species_dat <- species_dat %>% mutate(Database = "Species-level DB")
mag_dat     <- mag_dat %>% mutate(Database = "MAG-level DB")

final_dat <- bind_rows(mag_dat, species_dat) %>%
mutate(
Redundancy = DatabaseSize / N_Clusters,
PercentRedundant = 100 * (DatabaseSize - N_Clusters) / DatabaseSize,
RedundantProteins = DatabaseSize - N_Clusters
)

# ---- Gene-level annotation status (species-level only) ----

# "Unclassified" (and NA / "") treated as NOT annotated

kegg_pct <- gene_dat %>%
mutate(KEGG_status = ifelse(
is.na(KEGG_ID) | KEGG_ID == "" | KEGG_ID == "Unclassified",
"Not annotated", "Annotated"
)) %>%
count(KEGG_status, name = "N") %>%
mutate(Percent = 100 * N / sum(N))

# ---- Peptide inference reports (MAG vs Species) ----

species_rep <- read.table(
species_report_file,
sep = "\t", header = FALSE, comment.char = "#", stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
colnames(species_rep) <- c("Metric", "Value")
species_rep$Value <- as.numeric(species_rep$Value)

mag_rep <- read.table(
mag_report_file,
sep = "\t", header = FALSE, comment.char = "#", stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
colnames(mag_rep) <- c("Metric", "Value")
mag_rep$Value <- as.numeric(mag_rep$Value)

pep_metrics <- bind_rows(
mag_rep %>%
filter(Metric %in% c("mean_proteins_per_peptide", "pct_shared_peptides")) %>%
mutate(Database = "MAG-level DB"),
species_rep %>%
filter(Metric %in% c("mean_proteins_per_peptide", "pct_shared_peptides")) %>%
mutate(Database = "Species-level DB")
)
```

# Protein redundancy
```{r}
p_redundancy <- ggplot(final_dat,
aes(x = ClusteringThreshold,
y = Redundancy,
group = Database)) +
geom_line(aes(color = Database), linewidth = 0.8) +
geom_point(aes(fill = Database),
shape = 21, size = 3,
color = "black", stroke = 0.4) +
scale_x_reverse(breaks = c(100, 99, 95, 90, 85)) +
labs(
x = "Clustering threshold (% amino-acid identity)",
y = "Redundancy (Database size / clusters)",
title = "Protein database redundancy"
) +
theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
theme(
legend.position = "top",
panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
axis.text.x = element_text(size = 14),
axis.text.y = element_text(size = 14)
)

p_redundancy
```
# peptide redundancy
```{r}
p_mean_ppp <- ggplot(pep_metrics %>% filter(Metric == "mean_proteins_per_peptide"),
aes(x = Database, y = Value, fill = Database)) +
geom_col(width = 0.6, color = "black", linewidth = 0.3) +
scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::number_format(accuracy = 0.01)) +
scale_fill_manual(values = c(
"MAG-level DB" = "#d95f02",
"Species-level DB" = "#1b9e77"
)) +
labs(
x = NULL,
y = "Proteins per peptide (mean)",
title = "Proteins per peptide"
) +
theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
theme(
legend.position = "none",
panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
axis.text.x = element_text(size = 14),
axis.text.y = element_text(size = 14)
)

p_mean_ppp

```
# annotated
```{r}
p_kegg <- ggplot(kegg_pct, aes(x = "Species-level DB", y = Percent, fill = KEGG_status)) +
geom_col(width = 0.6, color = "black", linewidth = 0.3) +
scale_y_continuous(
limits = c(0, 100),
breaks = seq(0, 100, by = 10),
labels = percent_format(scale = 1)
) +
scale_fill_manual(values = c(
"Annotated" = "#1b9e77",
"Not annotated" = "grey80"
)) +
labs(
x = NULL,
y = "Proteins (%)",
title = "KEGG annotation coverage"
) +
theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
theme(
legend.position = "top",
panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
axis.text.x = element_text(size = 14),
axis.text.y = element_text(size = 14)
)

p_kegg


```


```{r}
# ============================================================
# 2) Data manipulation and treatment  (UPDATED)
#   - Protein redundancy: % redundant
#   - Add peptide sharing: % peptides mapping to >1 protein (from reports)
#   - Annotation: Annotated if KEGG OR COG OR TIGR (PFAM ignored)
# ============================================================

# ---- Redundancy data (protein clustering) ----
species_dat <- species_dat %>% mutate(Database = "Species-level DB")
mag_dat     <- mag_dat %>% mutate(Database = "MAG-level DB")

final_dat <- bind_rows(mag_dat, species_dat) %>%
  mutate(
    Redundancy = DatabaseSize / N_Clusters,
    PercentRedundant = 100 * (DatabaseSize - N_Clusters) / DatabaseSize
  )

# ---- Annotation coverage (species-level only): KEGG/COG/TIGR (PFAM ignored) ----
ann_any <- gene_dat %>%
  mutate(
    KEGG_OK = !is.na(KEGG_ID) & KEGG_ID != "" & KEGG_ID != "Unclassified",
    COG_OK  = !is.na(COG_ID)  & COG_ID  != "" & COG_ID  != "Unclassified",
    TIGR_OK = !is.na(TIGR_ID) & TIGR_ID != "" & TIGR_ID != "Unclassified",
    AnnotStatus = ifelse(KEGG_OK | COG_OK | TIGR_OK, "Annotated (KEGG/COG/TIGR)", "Not annotated")
  ) %>%
  count(AnnotStatus, name = "N") %>%
  mutate(Percent = 100 * N / sum(N))

# ---- Peptide inference reports (MAG vs Species) ----
species_rep <- read.table(
  species_report_file,
  sep = "\t", header = FALSE, comment.char = "#", stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
colnames(species_rep) <- c("Metric", "Value")
species_rep$Value <- as.numeric(species_rep$Value)

mag_rep <- read.table(
  mag_report_file,
  sep = "\t", header = FALSE, comment.char = "#", stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
colnames(mag_rep) <- c("Metric", "Value")
mag_rep$Value <- as.numeric(mag_rep$Value)

pep_metrics <- bind_rows(
  mag_rep %>%
    filter(Metric %in% c("mean_proteins_per_peptide", "pct_shared_peptides")) %>%
    mutate(Database = "MAG-level DB"),
  species_rep %>%
    filter(Metric %in% c("mean_proteins_per_peptide", "pct_shared_peptides")) %>%
    mutate(Database = "Species-level DB")
)

# Pull the % shared peptides into a small table for adding to the redundancy plot
pep_shared <- pep_metrics %>%
  filter(Metric == "pct_shared_peptides") %>%
  transmute(Database, PercentSharedPeptides = Value)

```


```{r}
# ============================================================
# 3) Figure: Protein redundancy as % redundant
#    + overlay % shared peptides as dashed horizontal lines
#    (both on 0–100% scale)
# ============================================================

p_redundancy <- ggplot(final_dat,
                       aes(x = ClusteringThreshold,
                           y = PercentRedundant,
                           group = Database)) +
  geom_line(aes(color = Database), linewidth = 0.9) +
  geom_point(aes(fill = Database),
             shape = 21, size = 3,
             color = "black", stroke = 0.4) +
  scale_x_reverse(breaks = c(100, 99, 95, 90, 85)) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 100),
    breaks = seq(0, 100, by = 10),
    labels = scales::percent_format(scale = 1)
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Clustering threshold (% amino-acid identity)",
    y = "Redundant proteins (%)",
    title = "Fraction of redundant proteins"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 14),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 14)
  )

p_redundancy


```


```{r}
# ============================================================
# 4) Figure: Proteins per peptide (mean)
# ============================================================

p_mean_ppp <- ggplot(pep_metrics %>% filter(Metric == "mean_proteins_per_peptide"),
                     aes(x = Database, y = Value, fill = Database)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6, color = "black", linewidth = 0.3) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::number_format(accuracy = 0.01)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("MAG-level DB" = "#d95f02", "Species-level DB" = "#1b9e77")) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Proteins per peptide (mean)",
    title = "Proteins per peptide"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 14),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 14)
  )

p_mean_ppp

```


```{r}
# ============================================================
# 5) Figure: Shared peptides (%)
# ============================================================

p_shared_pep <- ggplot(pep_metrics %>% filter(Metric == "pct_shared_peptides"),
                       aes(x = Database, y = Value, fill = Database)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6, color = "black", linewidth = 0.3) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 100),
    breaks = seq(0, 100, by = 10),
    labels = scales::percent_format(scale = 1)
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("MAG-level DB" = "#d95f02", "Species-level DB" = "#1b9e77")) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Shared peptides (%)",
    title = "Shared peptides"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 14),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 14)
  )

p_shared_pep

```



```{r}
# ============================================================
# 6) Figure: Annotated vs not annotated (KEGG/COG/TIGR; PFAM ignored)
# ============================================================

p_ann_any <- ggplot(ann_any, aes(x = "Species-level DB", y = Percent, fill = AnnotStatus)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6, color = "black", linewidth = 0.3) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 100),
    breaks = seq(0, 100, by = 10),
    labels = scales::percent_format(scale = 1)
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Annotated (KEGG/COG/TIGR)" = "#1b9e77",
    "Not annotated" = "grey80"
  )) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Proteins (%)",
    title = "Functional annotation coverage"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 14),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 14)
  )

p_ann_any

```


# money figure for publication

```{r}
# ============================================================
# One clean 1-row figure (3 panels side-by-side) with ONE shared Y axis style
# Panels (left -> right):
#   1) Fraction of redundant proteins (%) vs clustering threshold (NO legend, NO y-axis title)
#   2) Shared peptides (%) with mean proteins/peptide shown above bars (NO legend, NO y-axis title)
#   3) Annotated proteins (%) (KEGG/COG/TIGR; PFAM ignored) (NO legend, NO y-axis title)
#
# Notes:
# - Panel 1 has its own x/y scales (threshold on x). Panels 2–3 are bars.
# - All panels show y tick labels as 0–100 WITHOUT % sign.
# ============================================================

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(scales)

# -----------------------------
# Ensure PercentRedundant exists
# -----------------------------
final_dat <- final_dat %>%
  mutate(PercentRedundant = 100 * (DatabaseSize - N_Clusters) / DatabaseSize)

# -----------------------------
# Peptide metrics: % shared peptides + mean proteins/peptide label
# -----------------------------
pep_shared <- pep_metrics %>%
  filter(Metric == "pct_shared_peptides") %>%
  transmute(Database, Percent = as.numeric(Value))

pep_mean <- pep_metrics %>%
  filter(Metric == "mean_proteins_per_peptide") %>%
  transmute(Database, MeanPPP = as.numeric(Value))

pep_dat <- pep_shared %>% left_join(pep_mean, by = "Database")

# -----------------------------
# Annotation: % annotated proteins (KEGG/COG/TIGR; PFAM ignored)
# If gene_dat has no Database column, this will show species-only.
# -----------------------------
if ("Database" %in% colnames(gene_dat)) {
  ann_dat <- gene_dat %>%
    mutate(
      KEGG_OK = !is.na(KEGG_ID) & KEGG_ID != "" & KEGG_ID != "Unclassified",
      COG_OK  = !is.na(COG_ID)  & COG_ID  != "" & COG_ID  != "Unclassified",
      TIGR_OK = !is.na(TIGR_ID) & TIGR_ID != "" & TIGR_ID != "Unclassified",
      Annotated = KEGG_OK | COG_OK | TIGR_OK
    ) %>%
    group_by(Database) %>%
    summarise(Percent = 100 * mean(Annotated), .groups = "drop")
} else {
  ann_dat <- gene_dat %>%
    mutate(
      KEGG_OK = !is.na(KEGG_ID) & KEGG_ID != "" & KEGG_ID != "Unclassified",
      COG_OK  = !is.na(COG_ID)  & COG_ID  != "" & COG_ID  != "Unclassified",
      TIGR_OK = !is.na(TIGR_ID) & TIGR_ID != "" & TIGR_ID != "Unclassified",
      Annotated = KEGG_OK | COG_OK | TIGR_OK
    ) %>%
    summarise(Percent = 100 * mean(Annotated)) %>%
    mutate(Database = "Species-level DB") %>%
    select(Database, Percent)
}

# -----------------------------
# Shared formatting
# - y ticks shown 0..100 with NO % sign
# - no legend
# - no y-axis title (use plot titles only)
# -----------------------------
y_pct_no_sign <- scale_y_continuous(
  limits = c(0, 100),
  breaks = seq(0, 100, by = 20),
  labels = number_format(accuracy = 1)   # <- NO percent sign
)

fill_cols <- c(
  "MAG-level DB" = "#d95f02",
  "Species-level DB" = "#1b9e77",
  "MagLevel" = "#d95f02",
  "SpeciesLevel" = "#1b9e77"
)

theme_clean <- theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, size = 12),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12)
  )

# ============================================================
# Panel 1: % redundant proteins vs clustering threshold
# (NO legend, NO y-axis title, y tick labels without %)
# ============================================================
p1 <- ggplot(final_dat,
             aes(x = ClusteringThreshold,
                 y = PercentRedundant,
                 group = Database)) +
  geom_line(aes(color = Database), linewidth = 0.9) +
  geom_point(aes(fill = Database),
             shape = 21, size = 3,
             color = "black", stroke = 0.4) +
  scale_x_reverse(breaks = c(100, 99, 95, 90, 85)) +
  y_pct_no_sign +
  scale_color_manual(values = fill_cols) +
  scale_fill_manual(values  = fill_cols) +
  labs(
    x = "Clustering threshold (% amino-acid identity)",
    title = "Fraction of redundant proteins"
  ) +
  theme_clean

# ============================================================
# Panel 2: % shared peptides + mean proteins/peptide label
# (NO legend, NO y-axis title, y tick labels without %)
# ============================================================
p2 <- ggplot(pep_dat, aes(x = Database, y = Percent, fill = Database)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.62, color = "black", linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = paste0("mean=", number(MeanPPP, accuracy = 0.01))),
    vjust = -0.6, size = 3.6
  ) +
  y_pct_no_sign +
  scale_fill_manual(values = fill_cols) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    title = "Shared peptides"
  ) +
  theme_clean +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y = element_blank()
  )

# ============================================================
# Panel 3: % annotated proteins (KEGG/COG/TIGR; PFAM ignored)
# (NO legend, NO y-axis title, y tick labels without %)
# ============================================================
p3 <- ggplot(ann_dat, aes(x = Database, y = Percent, fill = Database)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.62, color = "black", linewidth = 0.3) +
  y_pct_no_sign +
  scale_fill_manual(values = fill_cols) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    title = "Annotated proteins"
  ) +
  theme_clean +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y = element_blank()
  )

# -----------------------------
# Combine side-by-side
# -----------------------------
(p1 | p2 | p3) + plot_layout(widths = c(4, 1, 1))
```

```{r}
# ============================================================
# Side-by-side figure:
#   p1) Fraction of redundant proteins (dodged bars) + legend
#   p2) Peptide non-uniqueness (no mean labels; print means)
# Scales:
#   p1 y-axis: 0–90 by 10
#   p2 y-axis: 0–60 by 10
# ============================================================

library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(patchwork)
library(scales)

# -----------------------------
# Shared settings
# -----------------------------
bar_w   <- 0.6
dodge_w <- 0.75

legend_labels <- c(
  "MAG-level DB"     = "Mags (3150)",
  "Species-level DB" = "Species (495)"
)

fill_cols <- c(
  "MAG-level DB"     = "#d95f02",
  "Species-level DB" = "#1b9e77"
)

theme_clean <- theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    axis.text        = element_text(size = 11),
    axis.title       = element_text(size = 12)
  )

# -----------------------------
# Print mean proteins/peptide to terminal (MeanPPP)
# -----------------------------
cat("\nMean proteins per peptide (MeanPPP):\n")
pep_dat %>%
  distinct(Database, MeanPPP) %>%
  mutate(Database = legend_labels[Database]) %>%  # safe mapping
  arrange(Database) %>%
  print(row.names = FALSE)

# ============================================================
# Panel 1: Fraction of redundant proteins (dodged bars) + legend
# ============================================================
p1 <- ggplot(
  final_dat,
  aes(
    x    = factor(ClusteringThreshold),
    y    = PercentRedundant,
    fill = Database
  )
) +
  geom_col(
    position  = position_dodge2(width = dodge_w, padding = 0.15),
    width     = bar_w,
    color     = "black",
    linewidth = 0.3
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = fill_cols,
    breaks = names(legend_labels),
    labels = legend_labels,
    name   = NULL
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 90),
    breaks = seq(0, 90, 10)
  ) +
  labs(
    x     = "Clustering threshold (% amino-acid identity)",
    y     = "Redundant proteins (%)",
    title = "Fraction of redundant proteins"
  ) +
  theme_clean +
  theme(
    # ggplot2 >= 3.5.0 way (no warning)
    legend.position        = "inside",
    legend.position.inside = c(0.98, 0.98),
    legend.justification   = c(0, 1),
    legend.background      = element_rect(fill = alpha("white", 0.7), color = NA),
    legend.key             = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA)
  )

# ============================================================
# Panel 2: Peptide non-uniqueness (no mean label)
# ============================================================
p2 <- ggplot(
  pep_dat,
  aes(
    x     = Database,
    y     = Percent,
    fill  = Database,
    group = Database
  )
) +
  geom_col(
    position  = position_dodge2(width = dodge_w, padding = 0.15),
    width     = bar_w,  # keep as-is; you can change later
    color     = "black",
    linewidth = 0.3
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = fill_cols,
    breaks = names(legend_labels),
    labels = legend_labels,
    name   = NULL
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 60),
    breaks = seq(0, 60, 10)
  ) +
  labs(
    x     = NULL,
    y     = "Shared peptides (% mapping to \u22652 proteins)",
    title = "Peptide non-uniqueness"
  ) +
  theme_clean +
  theme(legend.position = "none")  # legend only in p1

# -----------------------------
# Combine and save
# -----------------------------
(p1 | p2) + plot_layout(widths = c(2.2, 1))

ggsave(
  filename = "PeptideLevel_Inference_Complexity_A4.pdf",
  plot     = last_plot(),
  device   = pdf,
  height   = 11.69,
  width    = 9,
  units    = "in",
  dpi      = 300
)

```

```{r}
############################################################
## Annotation coverage summary (KEGG/COG/TIGR)
## - "Annotated" if ANY of KEGG_ID / COG_ID / TIGR_ID has a hit
## - "Not annotated" only if NO hit in all three
## - Treat NA / "" / "Unclassified" as no hit for each DB
## - Makes the same single-bar plot as your KEGG plot
############################################################

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(scales)
})

# ---- helpers ----
is_hit <- function(x) !(is.na(x) | x == "" | x == "Unclassified")

# ---- build summary (counts + percent) ----
ann_cov <- gene_dat %>%
  mutate(
    KEGG_hit = is_hit(KEGG_ID),
    COG_hit  = is_hit(COG_ID),
    TIGR_hit = is_hit(TIGR_ID),
    Any_hit  = KEGG_hit | COG_hit | TIGR_hit,
    Ann_status = ifelse(Any_hit, "Annotated", "Not annotated")
  ) %>%
  count(Ann_status, name = "N") %>%
  mutate(
    Percent = 100 * N / sum(N),
    Ann_status = factor(Ann_status, levels = c("Annotated", "Not annotated"))
  )

# ---- annotation text for the plot ----
n_total <- sum(ann_cov$N)
n_ann   <- ann_cov$N[ann_cov$Ann_status == "Annotated"]
n_not   <- ann_cov$N[ann_cov$Ann_status == "Not annotated"]
p_ann   <- ann_cov$Percent[ann_cov$Ann_status == "Annotated"]
p_not   <- ann_cov$Percent[ann_cov$Ann_status == "Not annotated"]

annot_text <- paste0(
  "Total: ", n_total,
  "\nAnnotated (≥1 of KEGG/COG/TIGR): ", n_ann, " (", sprintf("%.1f", p_ann), "%)",
  "\nNot annotated: ", n_not, " (", sprintf("%.1f", p_not), "%)"
)

# ---- plot (same style as your KEGG plot) ----
p_kegg <- ggplot(ann_cov, aes(x = "Species-level DB", y = Percent, fill = Ann_status)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6, color = "black", linewidth = 0.3) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 100),
    breaks = seq(0, 100, by = 10),
    labels = percent_format(scale = 1)
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Annotated" = "#1b9e77",
    "Not annotated" = "grey80"
  )) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Proteins (%)",
    title = "Annotation coverage (KEGG / COG / TIGR)"
  ) +
  annotate(
    "label",
    x = 1, y = 98,
    label = annot_text,
    hjust = 0.5, vjust = 1,
    size = 3.8,
    label.size = 0.25
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 14),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 14)
  )

p_kegg
```
